data.narrative:
Xtend is a Series B-stage defense and security company that has raised $70M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies across the Defense & Security sector—8 private entities and 2 public equities (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS)—with focus on 6 same-stage private peers including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using total raised as a proxy base, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $133M. The peer-median method benchmarks against six same-sector, same-stage comparables, which span $1.0B to $1.5B in valuation; the cohort median is $1.2B, reflecting robust capital formation in the defense tech space.

The two methods diverge materially, reflecting the tension between early capital deployment and peer-set benchmarking. Triangulation yields a range of $113M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $666M. The wide spread reflects uncertainty around Xtend's differentiation relative to later-stage, larger peers. A valuation toward the lower end would assume Xtend trades at a substantial discount to peer medians; toward the high end assumes catch-up to peer valuations. Resolution hinges on revenue traction, customer concentration, and defensibility relative to the stated peer cohort.
